target: The warp/weft model. Measurable time as warp (regular, structural), fateful time as weft (irregular, pattern-making). The fabric only exists where both meet. A simple loom diagram with §§6/14/20/22/25/28 as the sections where the weft-thread enters.

comparison.overallNotes: Candidate 1 provides the cleanest and most accurate visual metaphor of the simple loom diagram without relying on distracting text, and its weft entry point aligns well with the prompt's first numerical hint. Candidate 3 is a close second but slightly less precise in its weft placement. Candidate 2 is heavily cluttered with text, and Candidate 4 is too abstract and misses the structural requirement.

candidates.description: A clean, simple diagram of a loom with vertical warp threads and a single blue weft thread weaving through a specific section starting near the 6th thread.
strengths: Clear distinction between regular warp and irregular weft
Weft entry point roughly aligns with the '6' in the prompt
issues: Does not perfectly capture the exact multiple numerical sequences of entry points due to the simplified nature of the diagram
candidates.suggestedRefinement: Introduce additional, distinct weft threads entering at other specific sections to fully capture the numerical sequence requested.
candidates.notes: The strongest candidate. It perfectly captures the visual metaphor of the fabric existing only where the regular warp and irregular weft meet, maintaining a clean and legible diagrammatic style.

candidates.description: A complex loom diagram heavily annotated with text explaining the metaphor of time, with multiple weft threads entering at various points.
strengths: Attempts to show multiple entry points for the weft threads
issues: Relies on literal labels rather than visual symbolism to convey the brief
candidates.suggestedRefinement: Remove all text labels and arrows, and simplify the visual representation of the multiple weft entry points to let the diagram speak for itself.
candidates.notes: While conceptually on track, the image fails the negative constraint regarding distracting text. The labels act as a crutch and clutter the visual.

candidates.description: A simple loom diagram similar to Candidate 1, with the weft thread entering further to the right.
strengths: Clearly shows the fabric existing only where warp and weft meet
issues: Weft entry point is less aligned with the prompt's specific numerical suggestions compared to Candidate 1
candidates.suggestedRefinement: Adjust the weft entry point to start earlier, closer to the 6th warp thread, and perhaps add more irregular weaving sections.
candidates.notes: A very strong alternative to Candidate 1, losing only slightly on the symbolic precision of the weft's starting position.

candidates.description: An abstract, close-up pattern of woven threads, lacking a clear loom structure or frame.
issues: Lacks the structural clarity requested in the brief
candidates.suggestedRefinement: Redraw completely as a simple loom diagram showing the outer frame and distinct, countable warp and weft threads.
candidates.notes: This candidate misses the core requirement of being a 'simple loom diagram', opting instead for an abstract texture that fails to convey the specific metaphor.

candidate.text: The warp/weft model. Measurable time as warp (regular, structural), fateful time as weft (irregular, pattern-making). The fabric only exists where both meet. A simple loom diagram with §§6/14/20/22/25/28 as the sections where the weft-thread enters.
